title: In mathematics, an invariant is a property that does not change under any transformation. Precept's rules are invariants: no event, no data value, no code path can make the definition untrue. The circle is the governed domain; the bisecting horizontal rule is the invariant — it holds at the same position regardless of what exists inside.
conceptualBridge: The invariant line means Precept's rules hold unconditionally because the bisecting rule maintains its exact position regardless of any state change within the governed domain.
decision: Removed: avg 3.9 below threshold; circle+bisecting line is a generic silhouette that doesn't reach the distinctiveness bar.
